title: Canonical queue snapshots can lag additive implementation evidence
observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json records C-001 progress through the earlier deployment-readiness and proof pipeline.
Later commits add an exact-commit dispatch decision builder, deterministic tests, evidence, and an additive queue-update record.
The later implementation does not erase the historical accuracy of the older queue blob at its commit time.
inferred: Readers that consult only the canonical queue may understate current implementation progress.
Readers that consult only recent commits may lose the reason an older queue state was retained.
proposed: Continuity readers should reconcile canonical state with additive queue-update records and recent commits before reporting current status.
Canonical queue updates should be made only by the owning team or a dedicated reconciliation process that preserves prior blob provenance.
unresolved: The dispatch decision builder is not yet wired into the environment-preflight workflow.
No resolving Cloudflare deployment URL or served-commit proof has been observed.

included: Public repository paths, blob SHAs, commit SHAs, and implementation-state summaries

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch ACTIVE_QUEUE.json at blob abb807e1a25d399f301238f6b8cc26d6be10385f and inspect C-001 progress. Then fetch commits cf808e15ba3c7aa305aea20b0aac57812015cf7f, 5d4ad61c302fc7e4456fce78b6c8334880bf0c05, 7afbd0d7624be8e82130349465fa91d4cb27fbff, and bb085a68135b02502cad7897afd105c1489741f0. Reject this record if those commits do not add the stated builder, tests, evidence, and queue update.
handoff: Cloudflare Research should wire the exact-commit dispatch decision into preflight and then update its owned canonical C-001 progress. Continuity Mining should later link the resulting canonical update as a temporal successor rather than overwriting CM-0010.
